ddls:
CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "geo_coordinates" TEXT /* Geographic coordinates (latitude and longitude) for the region */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code for the country */,
  "geo_coordinates" TEXT /* Geographic coordinates (latitude and longitude) for the country */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "authors" (
  "authors_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each author */,
  "author_name" TEXT /* Name of the author */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution or organization affiliated with the author */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the author */,
  "orcid" TEXT /* ORCID identifier for the author */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("authors_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "journals" (
  "journal_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each journal */,
  "journal_name" TEXT /* Name of the journal */,
  "journal_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the journal */,
  "issn" TEXT /* ISSN (International Standard Serial Number) for the journal */,
  "eissn" TEXT /* EISSN (Electronic International Standard Serial Number) for the journal */,
  "publisher" TEXT /* Publisher of the journal */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("journal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "host_species" (
  "host_species_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each host species */,
  "species_name" TEXT /* Name of the host species */,
  "taxonomic_rank" TEXT /* Taxonomic rank of the host species */,
  "common_name" TEXT /* Common name of the host species */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("host_species_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the segment */,
  "segment_length" INTEGER /* Length of the segment in base pairs */,
  "segment_type" TEXT /* Type of segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mutations" (
  "mutation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mutation */,
  "strain_id" INTEGER /* ID of the strain the mutation belongs to */,
  "mutation_details" TEXT /* Detailed information about the mutations */,
  "mutation_type" TEXT /* Type of mutation */,
  "mutation_position" INTEGER /* Position of the mutation within the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mutation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "strain_id" INTEGER /* ID of the strain being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the strain */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the strain was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the strain */,
  "access_user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the user accessing the strain */,
  "access_session_id" TEXT /* Session ID for the user accessing the strain */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution or organization affiliated with the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department within the institution */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data file */,
  "strain_id" INTEGER /* ID of the strain the file belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the data file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the data file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "file_format" TEXT /* Format of the data file */,
  "file_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the data file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "readme_files" (
  "readme_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each readme file */,
  "strain_id" INTEGER /* ID of the strain the readme file belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the readme file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the readme file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "file_format" TEXT /* Format of the readme file */,
  "file_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the readme file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("readme_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "annotations" (
  "annotation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each annotation */,
  "strain_id" INTEGER /* ID of the strain the annotation belongs to */,
  "annotation_text" TEXT /* Text of the annotation */,
  "annotation_type" TEXT /* Type of annotation */,
  "annotation_date" TEXT /* Date when the annotation was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("annotation_id")
)
